@@ -2,7 +2,7 @@ {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-0lo","title":"Implement toolchain verification and installation on worker","description":"## Parent Epic: Automatic Toolchain Synchronization (remote_compilation_helper-ayn) ## Overview Implement toolchain verification and automatic installation on the worker agent. Before executing a compilation command, the worker ensures the required toolchain is available, installing it via rustup if necessary. ## Flow 1. Worker receives ExecutionRequest with toolchain 2. Check if toolchain is already available 3. If not available, install via rustup 4. Execute command with rustup run 5. Cache toolchain availability for future requests ## Implementation - `rch-wkr/src/toolchain.rs` with a thread‑safe cache - `ensure_toolchain` uses rustup minimal profile - Executor wraps commands with `rustup run` when toolchain specified ## Tests - Unit: cache operations - Unit: toolchain parsing + strip target triple - Integration: mock rustup output and install failures - E2E: add to `scripts/e2e_test.sh` scenario that simulates missing toolchain and logs install flow; ensure fall‑open behavior on install failure ## Logging - Log toolchain resolution, install attempts, and fall‑open decisions with worker id ## Acceptance Criteria - Toolchain availability cached correctly - Missing toolchains installed automatically - Failures fall back to local execution - E2E logs show toolchain decision path ## Dependencies - Protocol changes to include toolchain (remote_compilation_helper-o9s) ## Blocks - Toolchain sync tests (remote_compilation_helper-mio) ","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T12:13:33.233869712-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T22:32:45.533031464-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-16T22:32:45.533031464-05:00","close_reason":"Toolchain verification fully implemented: thread-safe cache, ensure_toolchain with automatic installation via rustup, CLI --toolchain parameter, fail-open behavior, command wrapping with rustup run. All 222 tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-0lo","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-o9s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T12:14:49.436995369-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]} {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-0w4","title":"Update transfer patterns for Bun/Node.js projects","description":"## Task: Update Transfer Patterns for Bun/Node.js Projects ### Context Bun/Node.js projects have specific directory structures that should be excluded from transfer to avoid massive unnecessary file transfers. ### Requirements 1. **Default Exclusions** Add these to default transfer exclusion patterns: - `node_modules/` - Package dependencies (reinstall on worker) - `.bun/` - Bun cache directory - `bun.lockb` - Binary lockfile (may need special handling) - `.npm/` - npm cache - `.pnpm-store/` - pnpm store - `dist/` - Build output (usually) - `.next/` - Next.js build cache - `.nuxt/` - Nuxt.js build cache 2. **Required File Transfer** Ensure these ARE transferred: - `package.json` - Package manifest - `bunfig.toml` - Bun configuration - `tsconfig.json` - TypeScript configuration - `*.ts`, `*.tsx`, `*.js`, `*.jsx` - Source files - `bun.lockb` - May need transfer for reproducible installs 3. **Pre-execution Hook** - Consider running `bun install` on worker before test execution - Cache `node_modules` on worker to avoid repeated installs - Hash `package.json` + `bun.lockb` to detect when reinstall needed ### Files to Modify - `rch-common/src/transfer.rs` - Transfer patterns - `rch-wkr/src/prepare.rs` - Pre-execution preparation - `rch/config/defaults.toml` - Default configuration ### Testing - Test transfer with large node_modules present - Verify excluded files don't transfer - Test package installation caching ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T01:35:57.646709088-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T01:59:58.473460671-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T01:59:58.473460671-05:00","close_reason":"Core exclusion patterns for Bun/Node.js complete: .bun/, .npm/, .pnpm-store/, dist/, .next/, .nuxt/, .turbo/, .parcel-cache/, coverage/, .nyc_output/. Pre-execution hook (bun install caching) is a future enhancement.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-0w4","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-r2f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T01:36:23.875642791-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]} {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-17q","title":"Fix broken 'rch config set' command","description":"commands.rs:726-730 prints 'not fully implemented' instead of actually setting config values. Either implement the feature properly or remove the command from the CLI until ready.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T11:37:06.154251039-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T11:53:10.653219201-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-16T11:53:10.653223119-05:00"} -{"id":"remote_compilation_helper-1dz","title":"Update worker requirements for Bun runtime","description":"## Task: Update Worker Requirements for Bun Runtime ### Context Workers need to have Bun installed to execute `bun test` and `bun typecheck` commands. This task ensures proper worker capability detection and graceful fallback. ### Requirements 1. **Worker Capability Detection** - Add `bun_version: Option` to worker capability struct - Probe for Bun installation during worker health check - Store detected Bun version for capability matching 2. **Worker Selection Logic** - When a Bun command is classified, filter workers to only those with Bun installed - If no Bun-capable workers available, provide clear error message - Consider version requirements (e.g., minimum Bun version for certain features) 3. **Graceful Degradation** - If `bun test` fails due to missing Bun, suggest fallback to `npm test` - Log capability mismatches for debugging 4. **Configuration** - Add `workers.toml` field for explicit Bun path override - Support for workers with Bun in non-standard locations ### Files to Modify - `rch-common/src/worker.rs` - Worker capability struct - `rch-wkr/src/probe.rs` - Version detection probing - `rchd/src/selection.rs` - Worker selection logic ### Testing - Unit tests for capability detection - Integration test with mock worker that has/lacks Bun ","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T01:35:52.378498849-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T01:35:52.378498849-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-1dz","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-r2f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T01:36:23.819208396-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]} +{"id":"remote_compilation_helper-1dz","title":"Update worker requirements for Bun runtime","description":"## Task: Update Worker Requirements for Bun Runtime ### Context Workers need to have Bun installed to execute `bun test` and `bun typecheck` commands. This task ensures proper worker capability detection and graceful fallback. ### Requirements 1. **Worker Capability Detection** - Add `bun_version: Option` to worker capability struct - Probe for Bun installation during worker health check - Store detected Bun version for capability matching 2. **Worker Selection Logic** - When a Bun command is classified, filter workers to only those with Bun installed - If no Bun-capable workers available, provide clear error message - Consider version requirements (e.g., minimum Bun version for certain features) 3. **Graceful Degradation** - If `bun test` fails due to missing Bun, suggest fallback to `npm test` - Log capability mismatches for debugging 4. **Configuration** - Add `workers.toml` field for explicit Bun path override - Support for workers with Bun in non-standard locations ### Files to Modify - `rch-common/src/worker.rs` - Worker capability struct - `rch-wkr/src/probe.rs` - Version detection probing - `rchd/src/selection.rs` - Worker selection logic ### Testing - Unit tests for capability detection - Integration test with mock worker that has/lacks Bun ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T01:35:52.378498849-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T02:39:23.872098796-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T02:39:23.872098796-05:00","close_reason":"Worker capability probing for Bun runtime is fully implemented","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-1dz","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-r2f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T01:36:23.819208396-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]} {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-1f5","title":"Add shell completion generation (bash/zsh/fish)","description":"## Overview Provide shell completion support for bash/zsh/fish in a way that is ergonomic for users across environments. This bead focuses on *installation and distribution* of completions (docs, setup/installer integration, idempotent install locations), and complements the dynamic completion engine in `remote_compilation_helper-77c`. ## Goals 1. Ensure completion scripts can be installed in common shell locations 2. Provide clear docs and setup guidance for enabling completions 3. Idempotent install (no duplicate entries in rc files) 4. Work in offline or restricted environments where dynamic completions are undesirable ## Scope - Use `rch completions ` output (from 77c) as the source - Install to standard locations: - bash: `~/.bash_completion.d/` or `/etc/bash_completion.d/` - zsh: `~/.zfunc/` + `fpath` - fish: `~/.config/fish/completions/` - Optionally integrate into `rch setup` or `install.sh --easy-mode` ## Tests - Unit: completion generation does not error - Integration: install script writes to target location and is idempotent - E2E: `scripts/e2e_test.sh` installs completions in temp dirs, verifies files exist and rc modifications are not duplicated ## Logging - E2E logs should include completion output size, install path, and whether rc file was modified ## Acceptance Criteria - Completions can be installed with clear instructions - Idempotent install verified across shells - Works without requiring dynamic completion mode ## Dependencies - Dynamic completions engine (remote_compilation_helper-77c) ","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T11:37:04.972457231-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T01:57:29.472228072-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T01:57:29.472228072-05:00","close_reason":"Shell completions fully implemented. Commands: generate, install, uninstall, status for bash/zsh/fish/powershell/elvish. Standard install locations supported with idempotent install. All 7 completions tests pass."} {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-1t9","title":"Fix compilation errors blocking build","description":"## Critical Build Errors The rch crate fails to compile due to API mismatches: ### Issues Identified: 1. **commands.rs uses ctx.style() but OutputContext has theme()** - Multiple locations call `ctx.style()` but the method doesn't exist 2. **ui/progress.rs uses ctx.theme().supports_unicode()** - Calling theme() method that exists but the API usage pattern may be inconsistent ### Root Cause: API changes in OutputContext/Theme not propagated to all consumers. ### Fix Required: - Replace `ctx.style()` with `ctx.theme()` in commands.rs - Or add a `style()` method to OutputContext as an alias for theme() ### Files Affected: - rch/src/commands.rs - rch/src/ui/progress.rs","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T00:32:13.034034289-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T00:33:56.457408053-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T00:33:56.457408053-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed by adding style() method alias to OutputContext at rch/src/ui/context.rs:370-375. Build now succeeds with only warnings."} {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-20k","title":"Add terminal hyperlinks (OSC 8) for clickable URLs","description":"## Overview Add terminal hyperlinks using OSC 8 for clickable URLs in supported terminals. Provide safe fallbacks for non‑TTY and opt‑out control. ## Goals 1. OSC‑8 links when supported 2. Fallback to plain text URLs 3. Config/env toggle (RCH_LINKS=0) 4. Avoid OSC‑8 in JSON/plain modes ## Implementation - Detect TTY + TERM support - Wrap URLs as `\x1b]8;;URL\x1b\\TEXT\x1b]8;;\x1b\\` - Provide `link(text, url)` helper in UI module ## Tests - Unit: OSC‑8 formatting - Unit: fallback in non‑TTY - Integration: ensure no OSC‑8 when `--json` or `RCH_LINKS=0` - E2E: `scripts/e2e_test.sh` runs a command that emits help links and logs whether OSC‑8 was emitted in TTY vs non‑TTY modes ## Logging - E2E logs should explicitly show link rendering decisions ## Acceptance Criteria - Links clickable in supported terminals - No OSC‑8 in logs or JSON output ## Dependencies - Output abstraction layer (remote_compilation_helper-u0v) ","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T12:24:50.333718999-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T23:52:30.623127875-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-16T23:52:30.623127875-05:00","close_reason":"Closed","labels":["ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-20k","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-u0v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T12:27:11.328989527-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]} @@ -88,7 +88,7 @@ {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-piz","title":"Epic: Web Dashboard (Next.js 16 + React 19 + Tailwind 4)","description":"## Overview Build an optional web dashboard for RCH with Next.js 16, React 19, Tailwind CSS v4, lucide-react icons, and Motion (formerly Framer Motion) for animations. The dashboard should surface the same operational visibility as \`rch status\`, plus historical charts and worker management. ## Goals 1. Polished, modern web UI with dark theme by default 2. Real-time worker status + build history 3. Simple install/start flow (\`rch ui\` or \`rch web\`) 4. Responsive layout for desktop + mobile 5. Minimal backend footprint (reuse rchd status API) ## Tech Stack (2026 Best Practices) ### Next.js 16 (App Router) - **Turbopack stable**: 5-10x faster builds vs Webpack - **React 19.2 support**: Full Server Components - **Cache Components**: Fine-grained caching with \`\` wrapper - **DevTools MCP integration**: AI-assisted debugging - **proxy.ts**: New middleware replacement for better type safety \`\`\`typescript // next.config.ts import type { NextConfig } from 'next'; const config: NextConfig = { experimental: { turbo: true, // Turbopack enabled by default in Next.js 16 }, }; export default config; \`\`\` ### React 19 Features - **View Transitions**: Native page transition animations - **useEffectEvent()**: Stable event handlers in effects - **Activity component**: Coordinated loading/suspense - **React Compiler 1.0**: Automatic memoization (no manual useMemo/useCallback) \`\`\`typescript // Using View Transitions 'use client'; import { useViewTransition } from 'react'; export function WorkerCard({ worker, onSelect }) { const { startTransition } = useViewTransition(); return ( ); } \`\`\` ### Tailwind CSS v4 - **Rust-based engine**: 10x faster than v3 - **Auto content detection**: No \`content\` array needed - **Zero PostCSS dependency**: Direct integration - **CSS-first config**: Use CSS custom properties \`\`\`css /* app/globals.css */ @import "tailwindcss"; @theme { --color-primary: oklch(70% 0.15 240); --color-success: oklch(75% 0.18 145); --color-warning: oklch(80% 0.15 85); --color-error: oklch(65% 0.2 25); --color-surface: oklch(15% 0.01 240); --color-surface-elevated: oklch(20% 0.01 240); --font-mono: "JetBrains Mono", ui-monospace, monospace; } \`\`\` ### Motion (formerly Framer Motion) Rebranded in Feb 2025, import from "motion/react": \`\`\`typescript import { motion, AnimatePresence } from "motion/react"; // Smooth worker card animations export function WorkerList({ workers }) { return ( {workers.map(worker => ( ))} ); } \`\`\` ### Lucide React Icons Tree-shakable, 1500+ icons. Use direct imports: \`\`\`typescript // DO: Direct imports (tree-shaking optimized) import { Server, Activity, AlertCircle } from 'lucide-react'; // DON'T: Dynamic imports (bloats bundle) // import * as icons from 'lucide-react'; \`\`\` ### shadcn/ui Components Copy-paste components with React 19 + Tailwind v4 support: \`\`\`bash npx shadcn@latest init npx shadcn@latest add button card badge progress \`\`\` Components are server-component friendly and use CSS variables for theming. ## Project Structure \`\`\` web/ ├── app/ │ ├── layout.tsx # Root layout with theme provider │ ├── page.tsx # Overview dashboard │ ├── workers/ │ │ ├── page.tsx # Worker list │ │ └── [id]/page.tsx # Worker detail │ ├── builds/ │ │ └── page.tsx # Build history │ └── settings/ │ └── page.tsx # Config view ├── components/ │ ├── ui/ # shadcn components │ ├── workers/ │ │ ├── worker-card.tsx │ │ ├── worker-list.tsx │ │ └── slot-gauge.tsx │ ├── builds/ │ │ └── build-table.tsx │ └── layout/ │ ├── header.tsx │ ├── sidebar.tsx │ └── status-bar.tsx ├── lib/ │ ├── api.ts # rchd API client │ ├── hooks/ │ │ ├── use-workers.ts │ │ └── use-builds.ts │ └── utils.ts ├── styles/ │ └── globals.css # Tailwind v4 config ├── next.config.ts ├── package.json └── tsconfig.json \`\`\` ## Pages / Views ### 1. Overview Dashboard \`\`\`typescript // app/page.tsx import { Suspense } from 'react'; import { DaemonStatus } from '@/components/daemon-status'; import { WorkerSummary } from '@/components/worker-summary'; import { RecentBuilds } from '@/components/recent-builds'; export default function Overview() { return (
}> }> }>
); } \`\`\` Features: - Daemon status + uptime - Total workers + health summary (healthy/degraded/offline) - Recent builds (last 10) ### 2. Workers View \`\`\`typescript // components/workers/worker-card.tsx 'use client'; import { motion } from 'motion/react'; import { Server, Activity, AlertCircle } from 'lucide-react'; import { Badge } from '@/components/ui/badge'; import { Progress } from '@/components/ui/progress'; interface WorkerCardProps { worker: Worker; onDrain?: () => void; } export function WorkerCard({ worker, onDrain }: WorkerCardProps) { const statusIcon = { healthy: , degraded: , offline: , }[worker.status]; const slotUsage = (worker.usedSlots / worker.totalSlots) * 100; return (
{statusIcon} {worker.id}
{worker.status}
Slots {worker.usedSlots}/{worker.totalSlots}
); } \`\`\` Features: - Worker cards with slot gauges - Filter by status/tag - Actions: drain/enable ### 3. Builds View \`\`\`typescript // components/builds/build-table.tsx 'use client'; import { formatDistanceToNow } from 'date-fns'; import { CheckCircle, XCircle, Clock } from 'lucide-react'; import { Table, TableBody, TableCell, TableHead, TableHeader, TableRow } from '@/components/ui/table'; export function BuildTable({ builds }: { builds: Build[] }) { return ( Project Worker Duration Status Time {builds.map(build => ( {build.project} {build.worker} {formatDuration(build.durationMs)} {build.exitCode === 0 ? ( ) : ( )} {formatDistanceToNow(build.timestamp, { addSuffix: true })} ))}
); } \`\`\` Features: - Build history table - Duration + exit code filters - Pagination ### 4. Settings View Read-only config summary with links to config files. ## Data Layer ### API Client \`\`\`typescript // lib/api.ts const API_BASE = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_RCH_API || 'http://localhost:7800'; export async function fetchStatus(): Promise { const res = await fetch(\`\${API_BASE}/status\`, { next: { revalidate: 2 }, // ISR every 2 seconds }); if (!res.ok) throw new Error('Daemon unreachable'); return res.json(); } export async function fetchWorkers(): Promise { const res = await fetch(\`\${API_BASE}/workers\`); return res.json(); } export async function fetchBuilds(params?: { limit?: number }): Promise { const url = new URL(\`\${API_BASE}/history\`); if (params?.limit) url.searchParams.set('limit', String(params.limit)); const res = await fetch(url); return res.json(); } export async function drainWorker(id: string): Promise { await fetch(\`\${API_BASE}/workers/\${id}/drain\`, { method: 'POST' }); } \`\`\` ### React Query Hooks \`\`\`typescript // lib/hooks/use-workers.ts 'use client'; import { useQuery, useMutation, useQueryClient } from '@tanstack/react-query'; import { fetchWorkers, drainWorker } from '@/lib/api'; export function useWorkers() { return useQuery({ queryKey: ['workers'], queryFn: fetchWorkers, refetchInterval: 2000, // Poll every 2s }); } export function useDrainWorker() { const queryClient = useQueryClient(); return useMutation({ mutationFn: drainWorker, onSuccess: () => { queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['workers'] }); }, }); } \`\`\` ## Styling & Theming ### Dark Theme by Default \`\`\`css /* styles/globals.css */ @import "tailwindcss"; @theme { /* Dark theme colors using OKLCH for perceptual uniformity */ --color-background: oklch(10% 0.01 240); --color-foreground: oklch(95% 0.01 240); --color-muted: oklch(60% 0.01 240); --color-muted-foreground: oklch(70% 0.01 240); --color-surface: oklch(15% 0.01 240); --color-surface-elevated: oklch(20% 0.015 240); --color-border: oklch(25% 0.01 240); --color-primary: oklch(70% 0.15 240); --color-success: oklch(75% 0.18 145); --color-warning: oklch(80% 0.15 85); --color-error: oklch(65% 0.2 25); --radius-sm: 0.25rem; --radius-md: 0.5rem; --radius-lg: 0.75rem; } body { @apply bg-background text-foreground antialiased; } \`\`\` ### Responsive Breakpoints \`\`\`typescript // Responsive layout for dashboard
{/* Content */}
\`\`\` ## Implementation Steps 1. **Scaffold** \`web/\` app with Next.js 16 \`\`\`bash npx create-next-app@latest web --typescript --tailwind --app --turbopack \`\`\` 2. **Tailwind v4 setup** + theme tokens \`\`\`bash npm install tailwindcss@latest \`\`\` 3. **Install dependencies** \`\`\`bash npm install motion lucide-react @tanstack/react-query date-fns npx shadcn@latest init npx shadcn@latest add button card badge progress table \`\`\` 4. **Layout + navigation** - Sidebar with nav links - Header with daemon status indicator - Status bar footer 5. **API client** for rchd endpoints 6. **Worker cards** + build table 7. **Motion polish** + empty/error states 8. **CLI integration** (\`rch web\` command) \`\`\`rust // Opens browser to dashboard pub async fn cmd_web(args: &WebArgs) -> Result<()> { let port = args.port.unwrap_or(3000); // Start web server if not running // Open browser webbrowser::open(&format!("http://localhost:{}", port))?; Ok(()) } \`\`\` ## Error States ### Daemon Offline \`\`\`typescript export function DaemonOffline() { return (

Daemon Unreachable

Unable to connect to rchd. Is the daemon running?

rch daemon start
); } \`\`\` ### Empty States \`\`\`typescript export function NoWorkers() { return (

No workers configured

Add workers to your config to get started.

); } \`\`\` ## Performance Targets - Dashboard loads in <2s locally - First Contentful Paint <1s - Time to Interactive <2s - Lighthouse score >90 ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Dashboard loads in <2s locally - [ ] Responsive layout works at 360px width - [ ] Clear error state when daemon is down - [ ] Worker actions (drain/enable) wired to API - [ ] Real-time updates via polling (2s interval) - [ ] Dark theme by default, consistent with CLI - [ ] Motion animations smooth (60fps) - [ ] All icons from lucide-react (tree-shaken) - [ ] Server components used where possible - [ ] \`rch web\` command opens browser ## Dependencies - Status API + build history (remote_compilation_helper-3sy, remote_compilation_helper-qgs) - Rich status data model (remote_compilation_helper-7ds) ## Tests - Unit: API client parsing - E2E: Playwright smoke tests (dashboard loads, worker list renders) - E2E logs: capture console + network errors ## Logging - E2E logs must include console errors, failed network requests, and render timing metrics. ","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T14:12:55.134604621-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T15:35:12.694862716-05:00"} {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-qgs","title":"Add build history tracking to daemon","description":"## Overview Add build history tracking in the daemon. This should record the most recent builds (success/failure, durations, worker, project), enabling `rch status`, TUI, and the web dashboard. ## Goals 1. In-memory ring buffer of recent builds (default 100) 2. Record start + end timestamps 3. Store exit code, worker, project, command 4. Optionally persist to disk for daemon restart survival ## Data Model ```rust // rchd/src/history/mod.rs use chrono::{DateTime, Utc}; use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize}; use std::collections::VecDeque; use std::sync::RwLock; #[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)] pub struct BuildRecord { /// Unique build identifier pub id: u64, /// When the build started pub started_at: DateTime, /// When the build completed pub completed_at: DateTime, /// Project identifier pub project_id: String, /// Worker that executed the build (None if local) pub worker_id: Option, /// Full command executed pub command: String, /// Exit code (0 = success) pub exit_code: i32, /// Duration in milliseconds pub duration_ms: u64, /// Build location pub location: BuildLocation, /// Bytes transferred (if remote) pub bytes_transferred: Option, } #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq)] pub enum BuildLocation { Local, Remote, } pub struct BuildHistory { /// Ring buffer of recent builds records: RwLock>, /// Maximum capacity capacity: usize, /// Next build ID next_id: AtomicU64, /// Persistence path (optional) persistence_path: Option, } impl BuildHistory { pub fn new(capacity: usize) -> Self { Self { records: RwLock::new(VecDeque::with_capacity(capacity)), capacity, next_id: AtomicU64::new(1), persistence_path: None, } } pub fn with_persistence(mut self, path: PathBuf) -> Self { self.persistence_path = Some(path); self } /// Record a completed build pub fn record(&self, record: BuildRecord) { let mut records = self.records.write().unwrap(); if records.len() >= self.capacity { records.pop_front(); } records.push_back(record); // Optionally persist if let Some(ref path) = self.persistence_path { let _ = self.persist_async(path); } } /// Get recent builds (most recent first) pub fn recent(&self, limit: usize) -> Vec { let records = self.records.read().unwrap(); records.iter().rev().take(limit).cloned().collect() } /// Get builds by worker pub fn by_worker(&self, worker_id: &str, limit: usize) -> Vec { let records = self.records.read().unwrap(); records.iter() .rev() .filter(|r| r.worker_id.as_deref() == Some(worker_id)) .take(limit) .cloned() .collect() } /// Get statistics pub fn stats(&self) -> BuildStats { let records = self.records.read().unwrap(); let total = records.len(); let successes = records.iter().filter(|r| r.exit_code == 0).count(); let remote = records.iter().filter(|r| r.location == BuildLocation::Remote).count(); let avg_duration = if total > 0 { records.iter().map(|r| r.duration_ms).sum::() / total as u64 } else { 0 }; BuildStats { total_builds: total, success_count: successes, failure_count: total - successes, remote_count: remote, local_count: total - remote, avg_duration_ms: avg_duration, } } } #[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)] pub struct BuildStats { pub total_builds: usize, pub success_count: usize, pub failure_count: usize, pub remote_count: usize, pub local_count: usize, pub avg_duration_ms: u64, } ``` ## Implementation ### Recording Hooks ```rust // rchd/src/executor.rs impl BuildExecutor { pub async fn execute(&self, request: BuildRequest) -> Result { let build_id = self.history.next_id(); let started_at = Utc::now(); let result = self.do_execute(request.clone()).await; let completed_at = Utc::now(); let duration_ms = (completed_at - started_at).num_milliseconds() as u64; // Record to history self.history.record(BuildRecord { id: build_id, started_at, completed_at, project_id: request.project_id, worker_id: result.as_ref().ok().and_then(|r| r.worker_id.clone()), command: request.command, exit_code: result.as_ref().map(|r| r.exit_code).unwrap_or(-1), duration_ms, location: if result.as_ref().map(|r| r.is_remote).unwrap_or(false) { BuildLocation::Remote } else { BuildLocation::Local }, bytes_transferred: result.as_ref().ok().and_then(|r| r.bytes_transferred), }); result } } ``` ### Persistence ```rust // rchd/src/history/persistence.rs impl BuildHistory { /// Load history from JSONL file pub fn load_from_file(path: &Path, capacity: usize) -> Result { let file = File::open(path)?; let reader = BufReader::new(file); let mut records = VecDeque::with_capacity(capacity); let mut max_id = 0u64; for line in reader.lines() { let record: BuildRecord = serde_json::from_str(&line?)?; max_id = max_id.max(record.id); if records.len() >= capacity { records.pop_front(); } records.push_back(record); } Ok(Self { records: RwLock::new(records), capacity, next_id: AtomicU64::new(max_id + 1), persistence_path: Some(path.to_path_buf()), }) } /// Persist to JSONL file (append mode) fn persist_record(&self, path: &Path, record: &BuildRecord) -> Result<()> { let mut file = OpenOptions::new() .create(true) .append(true) .open(path)?; writeln!(file, "{}", serde_json::to_string(record)?)?; Ok(()) } /// Compact the persistence file (keep only capacity records) pub fn compact(&self, path: &Path) -> Result<()> { let records = self.records.read().unwrap(); let temp_path = path.with_extension("tmp"); let mut file = File::create(&temp_path)?; for record in records.iter() { writeln!(file, "{}", serde_json::to_string(record)?)?; } std::fs::rename(temp_path, path)?; Ok(()) } } ``` ## Testing Requirements ### Unit Tests (rchd/src/history/tests.rs) ```rust #[test] fn test_ring_buffer_capacity() { let history = BuildHistory::new(3); for i in 0..5 { history.record(make_build_record(i)); } let recent = history.recent(10); assert_eq!(recent.len(), 3); // Capped at capacity assert_eq!(recent[0].id, 5); // Most recent first assert_eq!(recent[2].id, 3); // Oldest retained } #[test] fn test_recent_ordering() { let history = BuildHistory::new(10); history.record(make_build_record(1)); history.record(make_build_record(2)); history.record(make_build_record(3)); let recent = history.recent(2); assert_eq!(recent.len(), 2); assert_eq!(recent[0].id, 3); // Most recent first assert_eq!(recent[1].id, 2); } #[test] fn test_by_worker_filter() { let history = BuildHistory::new(10); history.record(BuildRecord { worker_id: Some("worker-1".to_string()), ..make_build_record(1) }); history.record(BuildRecord { worker_id: Some("worker-2".to_string()), ..make_build_record(2) }); history.record(BuildRecord { worker_id: Some("worker-1".to_string()), ..make_build_record(3) }); let worker1_builds = history.by_worker("worker-1", 10); assert_eq!(worker1_builds.len(), 2); assert!(worker1_builds.iter().all(|b| b.worker_id.as_deref() == Some("worker-1"))); } #[test] fn test_stats_calculation() { let history = BuildHistory::new(10); // 2 successes, 1 failure, 2 remote, 1 local history.record(BuildRecord { exit_code: 0, location: BuildLocation::Remote, duration_ms: 1000, ..make_build_record(1) }); history.record(BuildRecord { exit_code: 0, location: BuildLocation::Remote, duration_ms: 2000, ..make_build_record(2) }); history.record(BuildRecord { exit_code: 1, location: BuildLocation::Local, duration_ms: 500, ..make_build_record(3) }); let stats = history.stats(); assert_eq!(stats.total_builds, 3); assert_eq!(stats.success_count, 2); assert_eq!(stats.failure_count, 1); assert_eq!(stats.remote_count, 2); assert_eq!(stats.local_count, 1); assert_eq!(stats.avg_duration_ms, 1166); // (1000+2000+500)/3 } #[test] fn test_empty_history() { let history = BuildHistory::new(10); assert!(history.recent(10).is_empty()); assert!(history.by_worker("any", 10).is_empty()); let stats = history.stats(); assert_eq!(stats.total_builds, 0); assert_eq!(stats.avg_duration_ms, 0); } #[test] fn test_thread_safety() { use std::thread; let history = Arc::new(BuildHistory::new(100)); let handles: Vec<_> = (0..10) .map(|i| { let h = Arc::clone(&history); thread::spawn(move || { for j in 0..10 { h.record(make_build_record(i * 10 + j)); } }) }) .collect(); for handle in handles { handle.join().unwrap(); } let recent = history.recent(200); assert_eq!(recent.len(), 100); // All 100 recorded } fn make_build_record(id: u64) -> BuildRecord { BuildRecord { id, started_at: Utc::now(), completed_at: Utc::now(), project_id: "test-project".to_string(), worker_id: None, command: "cargo build".to_string(), exit_code: 0, duration_ms: 100, location: BuildLocation::Local, bytes_transferred: None, } } ``` ### Persistence Tests (rchd/src/history/persistence_test.rs) ```rust #[test] fn test_persistence_save_load() { let tmp = TempDir::new().unwrap(); let path = tmp.path().join("history.jsonl"); // Create and populate history let history = BuildHistory::new(5).with_persistence(path.clone()); for i in 1..=3 { history.record(make_build_record(i)); } // Load into new instance let loaded = BuildHistory::load_from_file(&path, 5).unwrap(); let recent = loaded.recent(10); assert_eq!(recent.len(), 3); assert_eq!(recent[0].id, 3); } #[test] fn test_persistence_append_mode() { let tmp = TempDir::new().unwrap(); let path = tmp.path().join("history.jsonl"); // First session { let history = BuildHistory::new(10).with_persistence(path.clone()); history.record(make_build_record(1)); history.record(make_build_record(2)); } // Second session { let history = BuildHistory::load_from_file(&path, 10).unwrap(); history.record(make_build_record(3)); } // Third session - verify all records let history = BuildHistory::load_from_file(&path, 10).unwrap(); assert_eq!(history.recent(10).len(), 3); } #[test] fn test_compaction() { let tmp = TempDir::new().unwrap(); let path = tmp.path().join("history.jsonl"); // Create history with 3 records but capacity 2 let history = BuildHistory::new(2).with_persistence(path.clone()); for i in 1..=3 { history.record(make_build_record(i)); } // Compact history.compact(&path).unwrap(); // Verify file only has 2 records let loaded = BuildHistory::load_from_file(&path, 10).unwrap(); assert_eq!(loaded.recent(10).len(), 2); } #[test] fn test_corrupt_file_handling() { let tmp = TempDir::new().unwrap(); let path = tmp.path().join("history.jsonl"); // Write some valid records + garbage std::fs::write(&path, r#"{"id":1,"started_at":"2024-01-01T00:00:00Z","completed_at":"2024-01-01T00:00:01Z","project_id":"test","worker_id":null,"command":"test","exit_code":0,"duration_ms":1000,"location":"Local","bytes_transferred":null} not valid json "#).unwrap(); // Should handle gracefully (skip bad lines or error) let result = BuildHistory::load_from_file(&path, 10); // Implementation can either skip bad lines or return error // Both are acceptable behaviors } ``` ### Integration Tests (rchd/tests/history_integration.rs) ```rust #[tokio::test] async fn test_history_recorded_on_build() { let daemon = TestDaemon::start().await; // Execute a build let result = daemon.client.build("cargo build").await.unwrap(); // Check history let status = daemon.client.status().await.unwrap(); assert!(!status.recent_builds.is_empty()); assert_eq!(status.recent_builds[0].command, "cargo build"); } #[tokio::test] async fn test_history_survives_restart() { let tmp = TempDir::new().unwrap(); let config = DaemonConfig { history_path: Some(tmp.path().join("history.jsonl")), ..Default::default() }; // First daemon instance { let daemon = TestDaemon::start_with_config(config.clone()).await; daemon.client.build("cargo build").await.unwrap(); } // Second daemon instance { let daemon = TestDaemon::start_with_config(config).await; let status = daemon.client.status().await.unwrap(); assert_eq!(status.recent_builds.len(), 1); } } #[tokio::test] async fn test_history_in_status_api() { let daemon = TestDaemon::start().await; for i in 0..5 { daemon.client.build(&format!("cargo build {}", i)).await.ok(); } let status = daemon.client.status().await.unwrap(); assert_eq!(status.recent_builds.len(), 5); // Verify ordering (most recent first) assert!(status.recent_builds[0].command.contains("4")); } ``` ### E2E Test Script (scripts/e2e_history_test.sh) ```bash #!/usr/bin/env bash set -euo pipefail SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)" RCH="${RCH:-$SCRIPT_DIR/../target/release/rch}" RCHD="${RCHD:-$SCRIPT_DIR/../target/release/rchd}" TEST_DIR=$(mktemp -d) LOG_FILE="$TEST_DIR/e2e_history.log" DAEMON_PID="" export RCH_MOCK_SSH=1 export RCH_LOG_LEVEL=debug export RCH_SOCKET="$TEST_DIR/rch.sock" export RCH_DATA_DIR="$TEST_DIR/data" log() { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; } pass() { log "PASS: $1"; } fail() { log "FAIL: $1"; cleanup; exit 1; } cleanup() { if [[ -n "$DAEMON_PID" ]]; then kill "$DAEMON_PID" 2>/dev/null || true fi rm -rf "$TEST_DIR" } trap cleanup EXIT mkdir -p "$RCH_DATA_DIR" log "=== RCH Build History E2E Test ===" log "Test dir: $TEST_DIR" # Start daemon start_daemon() { log "Starting daemon..." "$RCHD" --socket "$RCH_SOCKET" --data-dir "$RCH_DATA_DIR" & DAEMON_PID=$! sleep 2 if ! kill -0 "$DAEMON_PID" 2>/dev/null; then fail "Daemon failed to start" fi } stop_daemon() { if [[ -n "$DAEMON_PID" ]]; then kill "$DAEMON_PID" 2>/dev/null || true wait "$DAEMON_PID" 2>/dev/null || true DAEMON_PID="" fi } # Test 1: History starts empty test_empty_history() { log "Test 1: Empty history" OUTPUT=$("$RCH" status --json 2>&1) BUILDS=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(len(json.load(sys.stdin).get('recent_builds', [])))") log " Initial builds: $BUILDS" [[ "$BUILDS" == "0" ]] || fail "History should start empty" pass "Empty history" } # Test 2: Builds are recorded test_build_recording() { log "Test 2: Build recording" # Simulate some builds (may need to use actual build or mock) for i in 1 2 3; do # This depends on having a working mock build path "$RCH" build --dry-run "cargo build $i" 2>&1 || true done OUTPUT=$("$RCH" status --json 2>&1) BUILDS=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(len(json.load(sys.stdin).get('recent_builds', [])))" 2>/dev/null || echo "0") log " Recorded builds: $BUILDS" pass "Build recording" } # Test 3: History limit respected test_history_limit() { log "Test 3: History limit" # Record many builds for i in $(seq 1 150); do "$RCH" build --dry-run "test $i" 2>&1 >/dev/null || true done OUTPUT=$("$RCH" status --json 2>&1) BUILDS=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(len(json.load(sys.stdin).get('recent_builds', [])))" 2>/dev/null || echo "0") log " History size: $BUILDS (should be <= 100)" [[ "$BUILDS" -le 100 ]] || log " Warning: history may exceed limit" pass "History limit" } # Test 4: History persistence across restart test_persistence() { log "Test 4: Persistence across restart" # Check current count OUTPUT=$("$RCH" status --json 2>&1) BEFORE=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(len(json.load(sys.stdin).get('recent_builds', [])))" 2>/dev/null || echo "0") log " Before restart: $BEFORE builds" # Restart daemon stop_daemon sleep 1 start_daemon OUTPUT=$("$RCH" status --json 2>&1) AFTER=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(len(json.load(sys.stdin).get('recent_builds', [])))" 2>/dev/null || echo "0") log " After restart: $AFTER builds" [[ "$AFTER" == "$BEFORE" ]] || log " Note: counts differ (may indicate persistence not enabled)" pass "Persistence" } # Test 5: History file format test_file_format() { log "Test 5: History file format" HISTORY_FILE="$RCH_DATA_DIR/build_history.jsonl" if [[ -f "$HISTORY_FILE" ]]; then log " History file exists: $HISTORY_FILE" log " First 3 lines:" head -3 "$HISTORY_FILE" | while read -r line; do log " $line" echo "$line" | python3 -c "import json,sys; json.load(sys.stdin)" || log " (invalid JSON)" done else log " Note: history file not found (may use different path)" fi pass "File format" } # Test 6: Stats calculation test_stats() { log "Test 6: Build stats" OUTPUT=$("$RCH" status --json 2>&1) log " Stats from status:" echo "$OUTPUT" | python3 -c " import json, sys d = json.load(sys.stdin) if 'daemon' in d: print(' builds_today:', d.get('daemon', {}).get('builds_today', 'N/A')) if 'stats' in d: print(' stats:', d.get('stats')) " 2>/dev/null || log " (unable to parse stats)" pass "Stats" } # Run tests start_daemon test_empty_history test_build_recording test_history_limit test_persistence test_file_format test_stats log "=== All History E2E tests passed ===" log "Full log at: $LOG_FILE" cat "$LOG_FILE" ``` ## Logging - DEBUG: Log on record insert with build ID and duration - DEBUG: Log persistence operations - WARN: Log persistence failures (non-fatal) - INFO: Log history loaded on startup with count ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Recent build history available via `/status` - [ ] Buffer does not grow unbounded (respects capacity) - [ ] Persistence optional but safe - [ ] Thread-safe concurrent access - [ ] Unit test coverage > 85% - [ ] Integration tests pass - [ ] E2E tests pass ## Dependencies - `/status` API (remote_compilation_helper-3sy)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T12:15:56.044171161-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T23:24:17.640843684-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-16T23:24:17.640843684-05:00","close_reason":"BuildHistory and /status API fully implemented and tested - all 349 tests pass"} {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-qq0","title":"Fix WorkerPool len() and set_status() methods","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T08:58:32.833184859-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T09:07:01.026383152-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-16T09:07:01.026383152-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 4321639 - added RwLock for status, AtomicUsize for len(), all_workers() method"} -{"id":"remote_compilation_helper-r2f","title":"Epic: Expand RCH to Support Bun Test and Typecheck Commands","description":"# Epic: Bun Command Support for RCH ## Executive Summary Expand Remote Compilation Helper (RCH) to transparently offload `bun test` and `bun typecheck` commands to remote workers. This addresses the same problem as Rust compilation offloading but for TypeScript/JavaScript projects using Bun. ## Background & Motivation ### The Problem Users running 15+ AI coding agents simultaneously experience the same CPU contention with JavaScript tooling as they do with Rust compilation: - `bun test` can run hundreds of test files in parallel, saturating CPU - `bun typecheck` (TypeScript type checking) is CPU-intensive - Multiple agents testing different features create test storms - Local workstation becomes unresponsive during peak testing ### Why Bun? 1. **Speed**: Bun is the fastest JavaScript runtime (3-10x faster than Node.js) 2. **Growing adoption**: Many AI-assisted projects use TypeScript + Bun 3. **Same users**: Users with multi-agent setups often work on both Rust and TypeScript 4. **Similar patterns**: `bun test` is directly analogous to `cargo test` ### Why These Commands? **`bun test`** - Runs Bun's built-in test runner (based on Jest-compatible API) - CPU-intensive: parallel test execution, assertion evaluation - Output: Test results, coverage reports - Modifies: Only generates artifacts (coverage/, reports/) - Safe to offload: Pure read of source files + write to output dirs **`bun typecheck`** - Runs TypeScript compiler in check mode (tsc --noEmit equivalent) - CPU-intensive: Type inference, constraint solving, error checking - Output: Type errors to stderr - Modifies: Nothing (pure analysis) - Perfectly safe to offload ### Commands NOT to Intercept - `bun install` - Modifies node_modules (local state) - `bun add` / `bun remove` - Package management (local state) - `bun run
